subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: +1.3% | TSM hits TP1 · AVGO approaching stop

🔔 TP1 HIT. Today: TSMC 3nm price hike +15% H2 2026 + Nvidia AI partnership. Stock at $437.30 — $2.30 above TP1. Trim decision due. See Exit Alerts below.
Prices Jun 16 2026 · AVGO/TSM closes confirmed · NVDA/LLY intraday (±$2 uncertainty) · Sources: Investing.com, Benzinga, StockTitan, TradingKey, AltIndex
🚨 EXIT ALERTS
🔔 TSM — TP1 HIT · $437.30 (target $435)
Today's drivers: (1) TSMC raising 3nm prices +15% H2 2026 on surging AI demand, (2) Nvidia announced TSMC will implement its AI tech across manufacturing, (3) Intel CEO met TSMC management. Real resistance: prior swing high zone + analyst $435-440 cluster.
Sell 0.112 shares (50%) at ~$437.30
Gross: 0.112 × $437.30 = $48.98
Cost basis of shares sold: 0.112 × $388.35 = $43.49
Gross profit: +$5.49 · Commission: −$3.00
Net profit: +$2.49 ✓ clears $3 minimum
Remaining: 0.112 shares riding toward TP2 $467.84
Counter: 3nm momentum story isn't done — TP2 at $468 is one more leg. If you want to let it ride, trail stop to $420 and reassess at $468.
Your call. TP1 protocol says trim.
⚠️ AVGO — 1.2% FROM STOP · $383.50 vs $379
Earnings (Jun 3): rev +48%, AI rev +143%, beat-and-raise. Stock fell 14.44% anyway — pure institutional distribution at peak sentiment. ATH $495 → now $383.50 (−22.5%). Below 50-day MA $422.90 for 13 days straight. Stop $379 sits just above 200-day MA $361.80 — that's the real floor below.
If stop triggers at $379:
Gross proceeds: 0.315 × $379 = $119.39 · Cost basis: $129.79
Gross loss: −$10.40 · Commission: −$3.00
Net loss: −$13.40
Thesis (AI revenue) is still intact. This is momentum/sentiment breakdown, not fundamental breakdown. Rule: let the stop work. Don't pre-empt, don't add. Low-volume test of $379 = hold. High-volume break = exit on next bar.
📌 LLY — TRAIL STOP: $915 → $1,100
Stored stop $915 is 19.2% below market — offers zero protection. LLY broke $1,114 resistance on Jun 8 (you trimmed TP1 at $1,152). That level is now support. New stop: $1,100 (just below the breakout). Locks in trade at ~breakeven if stopped; gives room to run toward TP2 $1,243. Update via portfolio-manager skill.

💵 CASH DEPLOYMENT
Cash: $213.81 · Reserve: $6 · Commission: $3 · Deployable: $204.81
Option A — Hold as dry powder (RECOMMENDED)
VRDN PDUFA Jun 30 (14 days). If IBKR approves, current price $16.31 is actually better than the original $20 entry target — deploy the full $213.81 into ~13 shares of VRDN. Wait. Don't force deployment when the best play is gated by broker timing.
Option B — Add to LLY (risk 24/100, LOW)
Buy 0.18 LLY at $1,133 = $203.94 + $3. Blended entry: ~$1,020. Reasonable, but LLY already up 13.87% and TP1 already trimmed. Diminishing returns on concentration.
Recommended: Option A. Hold for VRDN/VERA when broker approves.
🔄 ROTATION
🔄 TSM TP1 TRIM → DRY POWDER
  • Sell: 0.112 TSM at ~$437.30

  • Remaining: 0.112 TSM still riding toward TP2 $467.84
  • Risk: 3nm story could push straight to $468 — you'd miss the second leg
Your call. TP1 is TP1.
⚡ RISK SCORES
TICKERSCORELABELDRIVER
NVDA24/100LOWAI monopoly intact; valuation only risk at 40x fwd P/E
LLY24/100LOWGLP-1 duopoly moat; stop needs trailing urgently
AVGO52/100HIGH−22.5% from ATH, below 50-day MA, 1.2% from stop
TSM33/100MODERATETaiwan geopolitical tail risk only; AI fundamentals pristine
📚 TODAY'S CONCEPT — #20 OF 30
Beta vs SPY
Beta measures a stock's sensitivity to broad market moves. Beta 1.0 = moves with SPY. Beta 2.3 (NVDA) = moves 2.3× as much. Today is a textbook case: TSM (beta ~1.2) rode the AI tailwind hard at +3.20%. NVDA (beta ~2.3) barely moved (+0.47%) — because its own 50-day MA is the governor right now, overriding the beta signal. AVGO (beta ~1.4) actually fell on a broadly green day — that's pure idiosyncratic risk overwhelming the market beta. LLY (beta ~0.5) barely twitched at +0.27%, exactly as a defensive stock should. The implications for position sizing: a $293 NVDA position isn't the same risk as a $112 LLY position. NVDA is 4.6× more volatile per dollar (beta 2.3 vs 0.5). Institutions weight by inverse beta to equalize risk contribution across positions — not by dollar weight.
Also today: Idiosyncratic risk — the company-specific portion of a move that beta can't explain. AVGO falling −22.5% from ATH while the AI sector is up = idiosyncratic (post-earnings distribution). Diversification eliminates this; beta does not.
Research only, not financial advice · Prices Jun 16 2026 · AVGO/TSM closes confirmed · NVDA/LLY intraday data (±$2) · Sources: Investing.com, Benzinga, StockTitan, TradingKey, AltIndex, Barchart · All held positions halal-verified May 11 2026 via Musaffa AAOIFI · VRDN/VERA/ARTV halal unverified · Next halal check due Aug 2026 · Last portfolio update: 2026-06-09
